claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

Let me start with a few basic questions:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm asking about my son Alex. He's 18 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 18 years old, I need to understand his current situation to determine eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he studying at school, college, or in an approved training program?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's still in college doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is 18 and in approved education, you may still be eligible for Child Benefit.

Let me ask you: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I live in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to understand your responsibility for Alex.

**Do you live with Alex, and are you responsible for him?** (This would include providing for his needs such as food, clothes, accommodation, etc.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. He lives here at home with me, and I'm responsible for him - I provide all his food, clothes, accommodation, everything he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Has anyone else already claimed Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, another parent or guardian?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question:

**Has Alex started any paid work for 24 hours or more per week, or is he receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he hasn't. He doesn't work that many hours, and he's not getting any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:

Even though Alex is 18 years old (over the standard age of 16), you still qualify for Child Benefit because:
- Alex is under 20 years old
- He's in approved education (doing his A-levels at college)
- You live with him in the UK
- You're responsible for him and provide for all his needs
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him
- He's not working 24+ hours per week or receiving benefits in his own right

**Important to note:** Child Benefit will continue as long as Alex remains in approved education or training and is under 20 years old. If his circumstances change (for example, if he leaves education or starts working 24+ hours per week), you'll need to inform HMRC.

Also, be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. If you're already receiving it, you should continue to be eligible under these circumstances.

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and still in approved education (doing A-levels at college). Under the Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16, BUT you'll still qualify if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. Alex meets this criterion as he is 18 and in college. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for him, providing all his needs including food, clothes, and accommodation. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Alex has not started paid work for 24 hours or more per week and is not receiving benefits in his own right. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex. Although Alex is over 16, he qualifies because he is under 20 and remains in approved education (A-levels at college). The claimant lives with Alex in the UK, is responsible for him, provides for all his needs, and no one else is claiming for him. Alex is not working 24+ hours per week or receiving benefits in his own right. All eligibility criteria are met.

Alex has not started paid work for 24 hours or more per week and is not receiving benefits in his own right. Therefore, the claimant meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

Although Alex is over 16, he qualifies because he is under 20 and remains in approved education (A-levels at college). The claimant lives with Alex in the UK, is responsible for him, provides for all his needs, and no one else is claiming for him. Alex is not working 24+ hours per week or receiving benefits in his own right. All eligibility criteria are met.
